pattern: Writing n as k consecutive integers starting at a means n equals k*a plus k choose two, so counting the run lengths k for which a valid positive start exists gives the answer.
hints: A run of k consecutive positive integers starting at a sums to k*a + k*(k-1)/2, so fix the length k and ask whether a valid positive start exists.
For each k, the leftover n - k*(k-1)/2 must be positive and divisible by k for the start a to be a positive integer.
Iterate k upward while k*(k-1)/2 is below n, counting each k where (n - k*(k-1)/2) is a positive multiple of k.

edge_inputs.description: smallest input, only the trivial single-term sum
a power of two has only the trivial representation
an odd prime always has exactly two

stress.description: assorted values up to a large bound
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, max(1, min(size, 1000000000)))]
stress.note: both implementations run in O(sqrt(n)) so the maximum is fast
